response.justification: The candidate has a long, strong individual-contributor track record (15+ years) with directly relevant developer-tooling and testing-infrastructure work: he built a test runner (Trial), an assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), set up GitLab/CI servers and DevOps deploy pipelines, and built a mobile device orchestration tool, plus shipped iOS/Apple TV apps. However, this is a people-leadership role (Level II Manager) requiring 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of formal people management, direct reports, performance reviews, or leading organizational change. The technical and tooling fit is good, but the core management qualification appears unmet, making this a borderline case.
strengths: Strong developer-tools/test-infra background (test runner, assertion lib, CI/CD, GitLab, DevOps)
Mobile-platform experience (shipped iOS and top-10 Apple TV apps)
Deep, broad engineering tenure with software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based, pairing)
concerns: No demonstrated people-management experience or direct reports
No evidence of leading organizational change or formal team leadership
Title progression is IC-focused (Senior/Staff Developer), not management track
